speaker: MATT
raw_text: That was great. So last we left off  The Mighty Nein, in making their way northward towards their eventual goal of Shadycreek Run to complete their second mission that they accepted from The Gentleman in Zadash, have been traversing the countryside and made their way to the city of Hupperdook, the largely gnomish cultural city there of industry and currently construction of war machines to send towards the conflict with Xhorhas to the east. There you saw the very hardy work atmosphere of the gnomes, and then the evening very crazy party atmosphere in which many of you dove into a hard drinking contest. You won the title of the Blushing Tankard Tavern, beating out their old champions, and woke up with terrible hangovers, and realizing that most of your gold had been stolen. Upon doing some sleuthing around the town, you had bullied a couple of bullies that were pushing Rissa around, they had been a problem for a while, sent them peeing their pants and rushing. You spoke with Rissa's father in his tinkering inventor's store, in which you discovered a shiny object, a fantastic experimental hand crossbow construct of his that he would be interested in relinquishing without payment if you do him the favor of correcting a mistake he made a while back. Apparently he crafted, on the request of the Starosta of the city, a powerful Gearkeeper, a construct-like entity that was meant to help be a warden within the prison that went crazy, killed a bunch of people and they sealed it off, and it's been there forgotten ever since. You said you'd take a look at this. Eventually you discovered that the money you had lost had been taken by four children, who currently are without parents, who have been living on the streets here in the city of Hupperdook while their folks had been sent to the prison six months before for being caught worshiping unapproved deities within the Empire. You, after having this conversation with them, retrieving your money, and leaving Kiri with them for the time being for safekeeping, decided to see if you had the possibility to prevent them from being sent to an Empire-run orphanage, in the hopes of freeing their parents in some way, shape or form from their binds. That was where we had left off.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The boxes are looking so cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I haven't seen the boxes yet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I probably shouldn't spoil that, but it's pretty cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I should see that. We're super excited about it. You guys have already blown it out of the water. If you haven't had the opportunity, now's the time to jump in and I hope you enjoy them. So I believe that's all the rest of the announcements we have.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm so emotional.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So let's bring it in to tonight's episode of Critical Role! [groovy Critical Role theme]

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And welcome back.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That was a good one.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That was great. So last we left off  The Mighty Nein, in making their way northward towards their eventual goal of Shadycreek Run to complete their second mission that they accepted from The Gentleman in Zadash, have been traversing the countryside and made their way to the city of Hupperdook, the largely gnomish cultural city there of industry and currently construction of war machines to send towards the conflict with Xhorhas to the east. There you saw the very hardy work atmosphere of the gnomes, and then the evening very crazy party atmosphere in which many of you dove into a hard drinking contest. Apparently he crafted, on the request of the Starosta of the city, a powerful Gearkeeper, a construct-like entity that was meant to help be a warden within the prison that went crazy, killed a bunch of people and they sealed it off, and it's been there forgotten ever since. You said you'd take a look at this. Eventually you discovered that the money you had lost had been taken by four children, who currently are without parents, who have been living on the streets here in the city of Hupperdook while their folks had been sent to the prison six months before for being caught worshiping unapproved deities within the Empire. You, after having this conversation with them, retrieving your money, and leaving Kiri with them for the time being for safekeeping, decided to see if you had the possibility to prevent them from being sent to an Empire-run orphanage, in the hopes of freeing their parents in some way, shape or form from their binds. That was where we had left off.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The boxes are looking so cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I haven't seen the boxes yet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I probably shouldn't spoil that, but it's pretty cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I should see that. We're super excited about it. You guys have already blown it out of the water. If you haven't had the opportunity, now's the time to jump in and I hope you enjoy them. So I believe that's all the rest of the announcements we have.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm so emotional.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So let's bring it in to tonight's episode of Critical Role! [groovy Critical Role theme]

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And welcome back.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That was a good one.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "It's been there for quite some time. A couple years at least, I think. Two or three years? So if anyone was, they've expired, I'm pretty sure. Sorry."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Would anyone believe that?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We technically have been hired to go in and take care of it. We're just working in trade.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Not by the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, they know not by the city, so by a private contractor.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I am sorry, I don't understand. You say they're too busy to deal with it. Have they got it locked away in a vault while it just stomps around?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "As far as I know, yeah. They'd originally had it placed in the bottom floor in one section which had more of the very violent criminals and killed a bunch of them. Not too bad. Killed a bunch of guards. That's pretty bad. At the time, they didn't have the manpower to fight it off, so they sealed the doors and haven't had a problem since."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: So was anyone else left in there with it? That it maybe didn't kill or--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's been the longest day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does your dad like pastries? Does he have a sweet tooth?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I imagine he does. I've seen him eat a few."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does he like pies?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Probably."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let's pick up your dad a pie on the way home.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Okay."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: People like pies, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I love a pie.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I mean, I like pies but I'm a bad example of so many things.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I really like meat pies.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Meat pies. Would your dad like a meat pie? I just hear people bring pies.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Fjord kind of leans in and goes, "That's good, Jester."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (snickers)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Beau. But that's okay. It's been a long, emotional day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Has been.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hasn't it, Fjord?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I have too much in my head right now, guys. It's been a long day.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Why? You playing five characters at the same time?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This brings me back to some home games.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Thanks, Baby Willingham.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh man. So much, it's been the longest day.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. Easy enough as you're wandering through the street, following the scents of food that are being cooked in advance of the current lunch hour happening. You hear a few whistles go off in the distance and in that moment, Rissa goes, "We best be hurrying before the rush hits." And you pick up the pace as you start hearing the different steam whistles go off and you watch a lot of the buildings begin to just swarm out with all their workers on their brief lunch break. You manage to stay ahead of most of the wave making their way up to the top shelf of the city, eventually coming to one of the vendors on the outside who is swarmed by about 12 or 13 different gnomes. You wait patiently for about a good 15, 20 minutes or so as they prepare and get all their meat pies ready, sweet pies ready. Sell a few things, a couple of their regulars cut in line in front of you, not paying much attention or caring that you're waiting patiently. But there is a large crowd behind you and you manage to get to the front and the two gnomes that are currently in the process of divvying this up look to the rest of you and go, "Oh, you're a right interesting team. What brings you here? You looking for pies? We got pies. You want pies?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I imagine he does. I've seen him eat a few."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does he like pies?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Probably."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let's pick up your dad a pie on the way home.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Okay."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: People like pies, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I love a pie.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I mean, I like pies but I'm a bad example of so many things.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I really like meat pies.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Meat pies. Would your dad like a meat pie? I just hear people bring pies.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Fjord kind of leans in and goes, "That's good, Jester."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (snickers)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Beau. But that's okay. It's been a long, emotional day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Has been.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hasn't it, Fjord?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I have too much in my head right now, guys. It's been a long day.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Why? You playing five characters at the same time?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This brings me back to some home games.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Thanks, Baby Willingham.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh man. So much, it's been the longest day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's been the longest day.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (chuckles)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We stop and get a meat pie on the way.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: A moment later, Cleff returns, clutching an armful of rolled pieces of paper and prints and parchment and goes, "Here's about everything I've got on the thing." Throws it down in front of you, some of it unrolls. There's various stages of design, the breakdown of the mechanics and the interior of this. It's extremely intricate and very impressive clockwork. Roll an intelligence check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Plenty! I built the thing myself. Don't worry!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: If you can sketch it for us so we can get a sense of--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, I've already got it. Don't worry there, friend. I'll be right back!" Turns around and disappears behind a--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We could steal the crossbow. We don't have to do the mission at all.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Please don't tell me that you're going to do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I could, just saying.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You hear some rustling and some things falling over. A couple of banging noises, and then you hear a (clattering), "Damn it."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that a cymbal crashing?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Being as the man's daughter is standing here with us, perhaps we will pass on that idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah, I can hear you, just so you're aware. I'm right next to you, little one."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She's clever.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, she just has hearing.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No. She's a wily one.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Secondly, a lot of the blueprints are in shorthand, and the design work-- while it's very beautiful to see-- the actual sketching of each stage of its construction and the interior mechanisms about it are very beautiful, it is very intricate. Paying attention to it and looking it over, the best you can ascertain is there is a core. There is some sort of a device that powers it. Through the notes around it, it's supposed to power for about 200 years before it needs replacing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, but I'm not going to be able to make heads or tails of any of this shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I have questions about it as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Caleb, you are the one who is the most proficient with understanding systems. If you could make an intelligence check for me as well, both you and Molly, since Molly was the first to grab them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Natural one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no-sies!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He's eating that extra meat pie.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Eight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You both take a moment and look over it. One, it's all written in Gnomish. Do either of you guys understand Gnomish as a language?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Does anybody understand Gnomish?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Gnome.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "It's been a while. It's starting to get a little warm." Rissa is like, "He's kind of a-- what's the term?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He totally just took a thank you for not his baby being born!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: This is the worst night ever!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Can I remove that?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Please take it off!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No! I’m committed!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I feel like at break time last time, it came off.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You made it to break, I think.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I've got two spares if I need it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Don't do this to yourself.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this point, as you guys are having this awkward conversation, Cleff is rushing around grabbing a coat and grabbing some other things, filling the inside of his apron with various tools and utensils, "Oh, I can probably use that. All right, I think I'm ready to come with you."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You all step outside, and you get about 30 or so feet before you see Cleff’s pace is slowing. He takes another step and keeps looking around.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: When was the last time you left your house, Cleff?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 18, not too bad. You take about five minutes or so to case the vicinity, and looking between the gate, you can see, two towers over, there is one partially constructed, wide bolt-thrower cart that only has one that's currently bolted to it. You see that they're taking leather elements and tarring them around the edges to prevent any sort of impacts from splintering the wood that's carrying it. It looks like from there, they go with a leather case and put metal over that and bolt it down. But in the process they do have what looks to be a couple of small pails that have some thick, black, tar-like substance that they're putting around the edges.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is that far from here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, it's on the northern side of the bottom shelf." She leads you guys over to the outskirts, and there is a big fence that wraps around the Assembly Yard, where a lot of these thin standing, skeletal towers of metal are placed against some constructs that are in partial levels of construction. Others are empty and not currently in use. Other ones seem to have been near completion, and they're in the process of preparing and breaking down the various siege engine for transport to the east. Who's keeping an eye out to see if there’s anything that could be used like tar? Who's looking for this?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll look for that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, make a perception check, please.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Ten. You take the ten minutes to look through, but this part of the gate is pretty solidly locked in. It's about six and a half, seven feet tall, and it's just metal bars with almost a cross-lattice of even thinner metal strips that are about a quarter-inch thick. The openings are about that big, so you can still see through like portholes, but it's not quite large enough for most of you to slip through. You might be able to if you're careful and squeeze, since you're small enough.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What's his range?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, let's see.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How far is it, Matt?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The nearest-- the one little part of the yard that they're currently using this material is about 60 feet away from you. 60 to 70 from your immediate guess at a glance.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it is about that distance.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So it might be just shy or-- let's give it a whirl. Just give me ten-ish minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ten minutes!? Oh, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's not bad. I pull a book out of my holster and flip to the right page and start muttering. Does anyone want to do anything in those nine minutes left?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll walk around and see if there's a way through the gate that's hidden.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'll keep an eye on the guard-- not on the guard, I'll keep an eye on the worker.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ten.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Ten. You take the ten minutes to look through, but this part of the gate is pretty solidly locked in. It's about six and a half, seven feet tall, and it's just metal bars with almost a cross-lattice of even thinner metal strips that are about a quarter-inch thick. The openings are about that big, so you can still see through like portholes, but it's not quite large enough for most of you to slip through. You might be able to if you're careful and squeeze, since you're small enough.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, let's see.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How far is it, Matt?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The nearest-- the one little part of the yard that they're currently using this material is about 60 feet away from you. 60 to 70 from your immediate guess at a glance.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it is about that distance.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So it might be just shy or-- let's give it a whirl. Just give me ten-ish minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ten minutes!? Oh, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's not bad. I pull a book out of my holster and flip to the right page and start muttering. Does anyone want to do anything in those nine minutes left?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll walk around and see if there's a way through the gate that's hidden.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'll keep an eye on the guard-- not on the guard, I'll keep an eye on the worker.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ten.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: There's the two large main doors, about 15 feet tall, about 20 foot wide, and then there's smaller doors, normal person-sized, almost gnomish-sized, doors built into it from that point. You actually watch as the human crownsguard has to duck a little bit to go through the smaller door frame. He's gone for about five minutes or so, and then returns and says, "The Wardenhelm would wish to speak to you, if you wouldn't mind stepping inside. Don't mind the escort; it's common business."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We are a contracted group of workers, working for this man right here, Cleff. We're here to take care of the problem that we heard you guys had with the clockwork warden.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cleff pulls the hood back a bit and goes, "Hello." Guy takes a moment and looks. Make a persuasion check with advantage, because Cleff is helping you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Persuasive Beau.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I stole this die that I got you from forever ago. It's got the little d20 inside of it. I'll take the 12, for 13.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The guard steps back a bit, looks you over. "All right. Hold tight a second, I'm going to summon the warden. Just a moment." He steps to one of the small doors that opens, and he disappears inside.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How many doors are there?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: He leans forward, and as the cloak is pulled back, Cleff reveals his face a bit. He goes, "Hello there. It's been a while. How are you doing, Poppin?" He's like, "Interesting riffraff you're bringing to my home this day."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Beau--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: First off--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I tap Beau on the back and make her bow a little bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You don't have to-- okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Respectful. I appreciate that."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Pull her back up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Rissa has backed away to the doorway and is like-- and doesn't want any part in this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Same, Rissa, same. We will not take up much of your time if you just let us in to go kill this clockwork warden that this guy made. We heard he was quite a problem. Just let us in; we'll be out of your hair, and you can get back to your work.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Wait, what are you--" And he looks over towards the cloaked gnomish figure to the side. "What?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Remember this guy?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: He looks at Cleff. "Is this true?" Cleff nods. "Actually, yes. I know it's been a problem for a while and I figured, about time I cleaned up that mess myself, if you don't mind." The Wardenhelm stops for a second and looks amongst the rest of you. "Right, okay. You have what appears to be an interesting crew of exterminators you've brought to me. What do you want from me? I assume you have come here for trade or for looking for some sort of recompense. I would like to know what that is before we move further into this. No one steps inside here wishing to do us, how you say? Charity work."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Remember this guy?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He leans forward, and as the cloak is pulled back, Cleff reveals his face a bit. He goes, "Hello there. It's been a while. How are you doing, Poppin?" He's like, "Interesting riffraff you're bringing to my home this day."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Why do people keep calling us that?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: To be fair, we are.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I was referring to Cleff! Cleff, why do you come and bother me with my day's work? What do you want? You're saying something about your little mistake, your problem?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Look, this guy is funding to take care of the spinning death machine that's in your guys' basement. This should be a city-funded issue, but this guy is being a good, noble citizen and funding it himself so we can come and make a quick buck and kill your death robot. Can we please just go kill your damn death robot?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: How is she out of spells? You guys took a long rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Any last tips, Cleff?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Be careful?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's a good one.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What kind of feet does it have again?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, it doesn't have feet. It rolls."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Ball bearings?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hamster ball.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We'll see. Slow it down. I don't know, we'll find out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. So the guards will take us down?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys currently have a group of six crownsguard from the outside and a couple of prison guards that will lead you there.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Tank this, and you'll be all right. It's just swords.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I want to get a good look at it, but this sounds like a fun challenge.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Fjord, you got a plan? Jester?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Jester goes, "Well, don't die, and if you fall over, I'll try to get you not on the ground anymore."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She can't. She's out of spells.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "About a little over two years ago."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Worth a shot.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A few other figures push their way to the face, and you can see men and women, you can see decrepit half-elves that look poorly nourished. You can see humans that look like they've smeared themselves with their own foul excrement. You can see a whole nature of people that are in the process of dealing with being locked down here in the subterranean basement level of this prison. And then, at the end, you come to what looks to be an iron door that is currently sealed shut, with what looks to be rung after rung after rung of chains pulled through, across bars, locked with three padlocks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is this the door?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The main guard sitting there goes, "Aye, it is." He pulls out a ring of keys.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: When's the last time you opened it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "When we sealed it."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Which was--?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. Frumpkin glances inside this busted cell and sees-- what I’m using as an interpretation of-- a large spherical metal object that's about eight feet diameter on any side. It looks like a series of overlapping metal plates that look clustered together at odd angles. Elements of it look scraped and dented. It looks like it's been doing some serious damage to it over time. As it sits there, resting, and Frumpkin approaches, you watch one of the tiny little metal plates (squeak) open, a lens pokes out towards Frumpkin, withdraws back in. The plate closes and about six of these long blade-like spider legs stick out of it as it pulls back and begins to roll forward at a very extreme pace.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Send the cat.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Sending the cat to take a look--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Scout with the cat. Kitty scout.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This may be the end of the cat.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, okay. I'm going to back as far away from that door as I can, and I will send Frumpkin in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Moving back in the corner with Beau and Yasha. Right about there, we'll say.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ladies.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You begin to focus and Frumpkin heads to the right side of the fork into the chamber you've already looked into, towards the open cell doors that are currently pried off that archway. As Frumpkin glances past, make a stealth check for Frumpkin.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yep. Oh. I don't have the cat paper with me in this state that I'm in, because I didn't know I'd be playing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So I rolled a 15.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Rolled a 15? Okay. LIAM. Yeah. Let's go with that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: 15 plus cat.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Good to know. That finishes Yasha's turn. It's now Fjord's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ooh!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay. So I get another attack?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's your first strike. You get a second attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So we now know at least a 17 will hit.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: That does not have advantage, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do, because you're currently flanking with Beau.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Because we're flanking, bitch!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: That's a 25.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 25 does hit. Roll damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Not an extra, because not Divine Fury?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct. Just 2d6 plus five.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. That's 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 11 points of damage. Nice! Yasha, busting it out. All right, that finish your turn?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just a reminder that everyone is rolling at advantage while the Cat's Paw holds it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: Ooh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, because it's restrained? Right!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Blinded and restrained right now.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Caleb, you heal seven points. That's a bonus action from Jester. What's next? You can only use a cantrip since you've cast a bonus action spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the paw reaches out once more to grab it-- that is a 19. 14 plus five. Sorry, man. You're still maintaining concentration, but it did not seem to grasp it at this moment. All right, that ends Caleb's turn. Jester's up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, don't do that! Go back. Sorry, keep going.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Should I try something offensive or healing-based, do you think? How are you doing, Caleb?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Bad.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so we're going to do this. We're going to run out and cast Healing Word on Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so step back through the door a little bit there. What level?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Level one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So a d4 plus four to Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes, 1d4 plus four. So that's seven points.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 33 points of damage in one round. Yasha doesn't mess around. That finishes your turn?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Now?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I don't know; is that bad?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know, can you get in there? Is the guts exposed?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is there enough gut to hit?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There are a couple of pockets where you can see inside of it, but you don't see the power source. It's risky.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'm going to wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you're just taking a strike with it? You have advantage because you're flanked with Beau.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think that's the best thing I've done this whole fight, is flanking.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Some fights aren't great for every class, I'm sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 19 hits! Roll damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: 16 to hit, so that's not bad.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep, 16 AC.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. 17 points.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 17 points! Carving past with the blade. You have a second strike, if you'd like.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I would like-- Does that also have advantage, or no?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah it is, because you're flanking.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay, ooh! 22.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that hits. Roll damage again.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 26, okay. As Yasha comes forward and slams her foot into it, it jostles the lock just enough for you to slip past the tumblers, and with a (creaks) it opens a few inches on the inside. The bottom of it is scraping against the stonework, the way it's been bashed and bent has actually forced it out of the frame of the door, and it's going to take a bit more force to push it open enough to move inside, but you manage to do so.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Caleb, I need you to come open this door.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to walk over to the door and start beating it with a sword.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, make a strength check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You can't dex cut this thing? You can't pop it in?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, you have to force it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yasha, I need you to come kick in this door.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: If this big thing couldn't kick it in, how could I?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm coming.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're bigger.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Eight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It is not budging.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're bigger in spirit and heart.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'll come and try to open the door.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You go over, see where Molly has failed, and you go and try to fill that void. Make a strength check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I believe in you. Oh, wait!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, go ahead.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's not going to budge.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to use this, Matt. I'm going to try to bend fate and try to repick the lock.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. It's a little late, but I'll allow it, sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Why not? Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a lot. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's 26.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. While Caleb's taking a moment to finish his short rest, you begin to peer inside and pull back some of the loosened armor plates. Now that the machine is open, there's not tension holding it together, and you see that each of these armored plates, on the inside, are attached to its own arm-like pole. Once it collapsed, all the armor plates went limp and fell open. It's a tension-held bit of armor, that it could expand and push out different areas to leave openings and then retract again. It's a pretty ingenious design, and you can see why Tinkertop is a talented man, though flawed sometimes in his designs. This is pretty revolutionary.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (slurring) I don't know, it might be yours. It's hard to tell.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (snoring)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Fjord pats you on the shoulder next to you and goes, "Hey, I just wanted to let you know, I appreciate what you did, taking the brunt of it, because it didn't even hurt me."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (slurring) That's very true. I'm going to remember that next time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "And that's because of you."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (slurring) You're the best, Fjord.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I know." All righty, so what else are you guys doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should we retrieve the magic core or anything as proof of what we did here?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I mean, the fact that we're going to leave the door open and it's not coming up, but yeah, we'll go get the core and I figure we're going to do a thorough investigation of the whole thing once Caleb can do a Detect Magic.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You do a scan of Detect Magic through the room over the next 15 minutes or so. You do a thorough pass around. Nothing magical catches your attention. Most of the interior here is-- Actually, no, that's not true. Very faint magical auras from two ember light torches that are on the wall. That faint red light that you've seen is essentially a very low light Everburning torch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's just part of the miniature. That was my representation for it. I did not have a mini of a spherical ball made of armor. I'm sorry, that's the best I could do for size and intimidation. I've been very busy, didn't have time to make an entire mini of a tiny spherical metal ball covered in blades.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What could you have possibly have been doing, Matt? Sorry!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, no. It's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Your mustache is traveling.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wherever it stays. Well, let's take this stuff back up to the warden?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We've had a short rest, and we'll do a quick search. Can you do a Detect Magic spell, Caleb?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I have a little bit left in me, Mollymauk. Will you help me up, please?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yeah come on, that's all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, he's my pillow! Fuck you, Molly.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Go fuck yourself, Beau. Come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (grunting) Okay. Point me at the thing.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: He accepts the coin, shouts out to-- he says, "Micahl!" And this female halfling comes rushing in. He says, "I would like for you to write a writ for me of success in clearing the lower regions of the prison under the watchful eye of the Wardenhelm Poppin Drokrusher. I thank you. And also, could we please have sent up immediately a," he leans forward, "Gilda and Wallace Schuster. They are on the second level in cell 12B. They have had their bail paid." The halfling rushes off, some of the guards leave with her, and about 20 or so minutes later you hear the footsteps return and there you see this couple. They pull forward, dirty, sallow, not very well-fed, but alive, and they both look bewildered. The man, who's got a thick graying brown scruff and the hair a bit shaken from his form, he glances up at the rest of you. "I don't understand what's happening."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: 150 gold?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 150 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Laura, you can text right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh no!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's okay. Laura will text if she has a problem with that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fjord can foot half, since he's sympathetic.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Fjord will pay half! So 75 gold each.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's true. MARISHA; Fjord says, "You know what? To keep them out of that orphanage?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Your gold, Laura, or your maternal bond. You choose!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Seven. Okay. So that's all right. Bump bump. Oh, and look at that! It's all done! Look at that, it's all done. That all happened!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Look at that. It's beautiful.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's like, "No money was spent!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's as if it never was!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What a bunch of assholes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's what you get for getting knocked up, man. Right there. Sorry. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this point, he--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Kids are expensive!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Kids are a deep financial drain, man!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's true!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Man, don't I know it.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: With that, you guide the now no-longer-prisoners outside of the Gearhold Prison, and as the small door opens to lead them out into the late afternoon air, the sunlight hits them. And you see them blink and wince, and then look up and smile, as this is probably the first sunlight they've seen in six months. You can see a bit of glimmer as tears begin to form at the edge of their eyes and the two, the couple, look at each other and smile, and hold each other, and look back at the group of you and say, "Thank you, but why?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's all right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We never understand what's happening, either.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Gilda? Wallace?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The woman looks over. She has this long, curled, and tangled brownish-red hair. She goes, "Is this true? We're free?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Why don't we get outside and then we'll talk?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's have a meat pie. (laughter)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: She takes you in there. Fjord steps forward and ruffles the feathers on the head and says, "You're always a member. Maybe one day, you'll be able to come back and use that dagger for something that's alive." Kiri turns back and looks to you. (like Nott) "Yeza was his name."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Go give Jester a hug. I know she wished she'd be here for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Jester, you see her arms twisted up and the eyes filled with water and she goes, "Oh! We're going to come back, right?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, we'll come back and check on Kiri. Be a good girl, Kiri.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She gives a big hug and goes, "Kiri, you're going to be so beautiful as you grow up, and this family is going to take care of you and is going to be your new mother and father and you’ll have this whole family here for you. Remember that no matter what's happening and what you are doing, the Traveler is always looking over you, and we are always-- always thinking of you with love." Jester turns around, and you can see the tears welling up in her eyes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I give Jester a hug.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Long rest to you all. The next morning you come to consciousness, your first morning not hungover for a lot of you, here in the city of Hupperdook. Now the city is yours to make your final business with.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Explains why your face is doing that, I suppose.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know what this emotion thing is. It's weird.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's not a good look on you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It probably isn't.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Come on, let's go find a quiet place to sleep. I'm exhausted.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Fjord, as you're walking along back to your inn, goes, "Now, I know y'all did this because I asked you to, and you weren't sure if you wanted to. I want you to know that's appreciated. You went out on a limb for me and these kids. I'll remember that. Anyway, come on." As you guys make your way back to a more solemn evening, the celebration is wild. The Blushing Tankard Tavern is in the same crazy energy and music you possibly can expect, but you are too exhausted emotionally and physically. You get a drink, find your way back to your room, and crash out hard.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Long rest.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Long rest to you all. The next morning you come to consciousness, your first morning not hungover for a lot of you, here in the city of Hupperdook. Now the city is yours to make your final business with.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Explains why your face is doing that, I suppose.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know what this emotion thing is. It's weird.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's not a good look on you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It probably isn't.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Come on, let's go find a quiet place to sleep. I'm exhausted.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Fjord, as you're walking along back to your inn, goes, "Now, I know y'all did this because I asked you to, and you weren't sure if you wanted to. I want you to know that's appreciated. You went out on a limb for me and these kids. I'll remember that. Anyway, come on." As you guys make your way back to a more solemn evening, the celebration is wild. The Blushing Tankard Tavern is in the same crazy energy and music you possibly can expect, but you are too exhausted emotionally and physically. You get a drink, find your way back to your room, and crash out hard.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Long rest.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At this point-- well, no, you slept through the night and you've woken up the next morning, so it's pushing close to midday.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Why would you think that?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, you tend to go mum for a while, and then you're gone. You’re very useful for us! We could do great things together, the not-nine of us. I hope you're not thinking of leaving.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Well, I'm not at this moment. I think-- and if I did, I would find my way back to you. Sometimes things need to be done.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, no one's going to bully you into a decision, but we're all happy to have you here. You know, my muscles are big. They can accomplish a lot, but we do better with you around.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Thank you, Caleb. I think you can manage just fine, but I will-- I'm here for now.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She's not going anywhere! What are you, crazy, Caleb? She's fine! Come on!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It was just a feeling I had.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're crazy!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I understand.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's fine!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What a strange feeling.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What time is it?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. You guys take your second watch. Fjord and Yasha are currently situated there. Two of you are up. It's now pushing past maybe one or two in the morning. The fire is burned low to embers at this point, and you're both staying close for heat. Fjord's sitting there looking, and goes, "I'm kind of proud of all this, you know?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Nott and I.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Nott and Caleb, if you wouldn't mind rolling a perception check with advantage, or both of you roll it individually.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: With advantage?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, he rolled, and now you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I got a nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You got-- okay. Here comes. Oh, big eight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. You guys take your first watch. Nothing. Though you're not paying much attention, nothing seems to transpire during that first watch period. You finish it without issue. The second watch come up. Fjord goes ahead and says, "I'll take it."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'll join you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You'll join as well, Yasha?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "But I’m awake now." Fjord goes like, "Let’s take a walk." Fjord stands up, Jester stands up, and the three of you step away for a minute. Fjord looks up at the stars and goes, "Dreams’ve been weird. For all the visions, the darkness out there, it’s good to know that, somehow maybe we're all putting a little bit of light in there too."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'll join you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You'll join as well, Yasha?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. You guys take your second watch. Fjord and Yasha are currently situated there. Two of you are up. It's now pushing past maybe one or two in the morning. The fire is burned low to embers at this point, and you're both staying close for heat. Fjord's sitting there looking, and goes, "I'm kind of proud of all this, you know?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I am, too. I'm actually very surprised at the choices we've made. Especially the choices that I've made.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah. A lot of these folks have surprised me." A little voice goes like, (as Jester) "Some people are trying to sleep."

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Sorry, Jester.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: In that moment, as you try and scream out, your vocal cords strained by the sheer force of your voice, no sound comes out. Looking up in the sky, there isn’t a single cloud in sight. Probably for the first time in weeks, since you’ve been in this side of Wildemount, it is a clear night. No sign of clouds. No sign of storm. In that moment, as you’re screaming out, the gag gets put into your mouth and tied behind your head. You glance over and you can see Jester being dragged. Now the manacles affixed to the back of her ankles and she’s being pulled through the grass, Fjord a few feet behind her. Still unable to really make out the shapes of these hooded figures that are pulling them through and you are shoved to the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Where was this?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She’s a bad motherfucker.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 18 damage. Oof! You have a second attack, if you like.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that’s a nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Your first one impacts one guy and there’s a splash of blood, silent. Once again, no sound here. The second one swings wide and the other guy captures it. He grabs your arm and pulls you in. He’s going to make an athletics check against you. Make an athletics or acrobatics check to try and not be grappled.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That’s not going to make it. He grapples and holds you in place. The other one takes and slams manacles onto your wrists and pulls it back, wrenching the blade from your grasp. Your arms are now locked behind you into these metal manacles.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Can I try to scream out in some way to the Stormlord?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You go ahead and scream. Make a straight wisdom check.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Three.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right, we're going to go so I can go to sleep because it's been a very long day. TALIESIN; It's been a very long day!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It’s a little much.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I be Terence Stamp?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It’s true.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Bless you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Thank you, Liam, for making it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you, Liam!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Thank you, Matt for making this game work with half of a crew.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: --Baryshnikov of Dungeons & Dragons.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Congrats to Laura and Travis!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I’m sure they’re sound asleep right now. Just getting a good night’s sleep. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah, the dads get to laugh now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Never again.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The first night is rough!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just you wait, Travis Willingham! The chickens are all coming home to roost!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This is the most complicated revenge plan I've ever heard. MARISHA; I know! These fucking assholes are like, "One of us! When are you going to join our misery?!" And I'm like, "No!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We're good!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Not misery.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Sleep is all gone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, it's not misery. It's beautiful and amazing and incredible.
